summary: A high‑quality soundboard capture of a scorching 1982 show, highlighted by blistering Let It Grow and an iconic Scarlet > Fire.
review: The recording delivers a clear, well‑balanced soundboard image of one of the band’s peak ’82 performances.  The night is famed for its relentless energy – Let It Grow, Scarlet > Fire, Sailor > Saint and Truckin’/Stella Blue all soar – even though a few releases suffer from mix quirks that bury Jerry’s lead at times.  Overall the show feels tight, dynamic and unmistakably “hot”.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance (many 5‑star comments) while noting only minor mix issues; recording is described as crisp soundboard, giving a strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and clear for a 1982 cassette‑sourced board.  Several reviewers note level spikes, a heavy Brent keyboard presence and occasional low Jerry guitar levels, but no major dropouts or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set rides a tight, driving groove with the Let It Grow/Scarlet > Fire transition often singled out as magical; pacing stays high‑energy throughout and the second‑set segues feel seamless.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is repeatedly called “spectacular”, “out of his head” and a key driver of Let It Grow, Scarlet > Fire and other jams; a few mixes bury his guitar but the performance itself is praised.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocals are highlighted as strong and clear, especially on Scarlet > Fire and in the second‑set vocal sections.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (and/or Keith) receives praise for soaring solos and a “awesome” keyboard sound, though some mixes make his level dominate the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann are lauded for a tight, powerful groove, especially the pre‑drums section of the second set.
